texts.module.generic:
Section:
{0}

Purpose:
This module generates Cisco IOS/IOS-XE configuration commands for this area.

Usage:
Enable the module, complete the fields, and then generate the configuration using Copy or TXT Export.

Note:
Drop-downs restrict fixed option values. IP addresses, interfaces, VLAN lists, ACL names, and descriptions remain free text.
texts.field.noDescription: No detailed description is available. Complete this field only when the corresponding command is required for the target configuration.
texts.field.separatorDescription: Each line is a separate entry. Columns are separated with |.
texts.command.more: • ... {0} additional commands are available in the Commands tab.
texts.optionalPart.description: optional command component; use it only when required or supported by IOS.
texts.parameter.choice: Selection value. One of the listed values is inserted, for example {0}.

moduleHelp.basic:
Purpose:
Configure the device identity, baseline protection, and time zone.

Generated commands:
hostname, ip domain-name, enable secret, no ip domain-lookup, service password-encryption, clock timezone, and clock summer-time.

Note:
The domain name is particularly important for SSH/RSA key generation. Clock settings intentionally belong to the base section.
moduleHelp.routingBase:
Purpose:
Configure global routing functions.

Generated commands:
ip routing or no ip routing.

Note:
This command is especially relevant on Layer 3 switches and is no longer imported as a custom command.
moduleHelp.ssh:
Purpose:
Configure SSH access and line vty centrally.

Generated commands:
crypto key generate rsa, ip ssh version 2, line vty, login local/AAA, transport input ssh, and exec-timeout.

Important:
VTY configuration is generated only here to prevent duplicate line-vty sections.
moduleHelp.aaa:
Purpose:
Prepare authentication and authorization.

Generated commands:
aaa new-model, aaa authentication login default, and aaa authorization exec default.

Note:
Plan local users as a fallback when using RADIUS or TACACS+.
moduleHelp.vlans:
Purpose:
Configure VLANs and SVIs without VRF.

Generated commands:
vlan, name, interface vlan, ip address, and ipv6 address.

Note:
Use VLAN / SVI VRF instead when VRF mode is enabled.
moduleHelp.vrfDefs:
Purpose:
Define VRFs as separate routing tables.

Generated commands:
ip vrf <Name>, rd <ASN:Number>, and description.

Note:
Other VRF modules reference these names exactly.
moduleHelp.vrfSvi:
Purpose:
Assign SVIs to a VRF.

Generated commands:
interface vlan, ip vrf forwarding, ip address, and ipv6 address.

Important:
ip vrf forwarding must appear before ip address.
moduleHelp.vrfStaticRoutes:
Purpose:
Generate static IPv4 and IPv6 routes per VRF.

Generated commands:
ip route vrf ... and ipv6 route vrf ...
moduleHelp.ospf:
Purpose:
Configure OSPFv2 for IPv4 without VRF.

Generated commands:
router ospf, router-id, log-adjacency-changes, network ... area ..., or interface-based ip ospf <Process> area <Area>.

Note:
The configuration method can be process-based, interface-based, or mixed.
moduleHelp.stpExtended:
Purpose:
Provide dynamic Spanning Tree configuration for PVST+, Rapid-PVST+, and MST. Only settings valid for the selected mode are displayed and exported.

Generated commands:
Global STP options, VLAN root/priority/timers, interface profiles, PortFast, BPDU Guard/Filter, Root/Loop Guard, link type, cost, port priority, MST regions/instances, errdisable recovery, and reset commands.

Validation:
Priority increments, VLAN/MST assignments, guard conflicts, Port-Channel consistency, MST region comparison, and platform guidance.

Live preview:
The generated STP section is displayed directly in the module.
moduleHelp.bgp:
Purpose:
Configure BGP for IPv4/IPv6 without VRF.

Generated commands:
router bgp, neighbor remote-as, network, and address-family ipv6.
moduleHelp.nat:
Purpose:
Configure NAT/PAT on routers.

Generated commands:
ip nat inside/outside and ip nat inside source ...

Important:
Inside and outside interfaces must be assigned correctly.

parameterHelp.hostname: Device name used in the Cisco prompt and logs.
parameterHelp.domain: DNS domain name; relevant for SSH/RSA key generation.
parameterHelp.secret: Secret key or password; do not document it in plain text.
parameterHelp.password: Password value for a login, line, or parser view.
parameterHelp.interface: Cisco interface, for example GigabitEthernet0/1, Vlan99, or Loopback0.
parameterHelp.interfaceRange: Interface range, for example Gi0/1-24 or Fa0/1,Fa0/3.
parameterHelp.ip: IPv4/IPv6 address or reachable next hop, depending on the command.
parameterHelp.wildcard: OSPF or ACL wildcard mask, for example 0.0.0.255.
parameterHelp.voiceVlan: Voice VLAN for IP phones; configured with switchport voice vlan.
parameterHelp.dataVlan: Data VLAN for clients or PCs connected behind a phone.
parameterHelp.providerVlan: Outer service/provider VLAN used by QinQ.
parameterHelp.customerVlan: Inner customer VLAN used by QinQ.
parameterHelp.tftp: TFTP or CallManager address used for phone provisioning.
parameterHelp.vlan: VLAN ID from 1 to 4094, depending on platform and design.
parameterHelp.vrf: VRF/routing-table name; it must exactly match the VRF definition.
parameterHelp.rd: Route distinguisher, typically in ASN:number format.
parameterHelp.routeTarget: Route target/extended community, typically in ASN:number format.
parameterHelp.neighbor: Neighbor address, for example an LDP or BGP peer IP.
parameterHelp.label: MPLS label or label protocol, depending on the command.
parameterHelp.ospfProcess: Local OSPF process ID; it does not have to match between devices.
parameterHelp.routerId: Unique router ID written in IPv4-address format.
parameterHelp.acl: ACL name or number that is created or referenced elsewhere.
parameterHelp.routeMap: Route-map name used for policy routing, NAT exemption, or route filtering.
parameterHelp.prefixList: Prefix-list name used for route filtering.
parameterHelp.port: TCP/UDP port or service, for example 22, 80, 443, or 53.
parameterHelp.protocol: Protocol value, for example ip, tcp, udp, icmp, ospf, or an inspection protocol.
parameterHelp.source: Source address, source network, or source object.
parameterHelp.destination: Destination address, destination network, or destination object.
parameterHelp.zone: Security zone used by the zone-based firewall.
parameterHelp.classMap: Class-map name used for QoS or firewall inspection.
parameterHelp.policyMap: Policy-map name used for QoS or firewall inspection.
parameterHelp.transformSet: IPsec transform set containing encryption and hash parameters.
parameterHelp.bytes: Storage or buffer size in bytes.
parameterHelp.level: Privilege, logging, or security level, depending on the command.
parameterHelp.priority: Priority or sequence number; lower values are often preferred.
parameterHelp.percent: Percentage used for bandwidth or QoS reservation.
parameterHelp.dscp: DSCP value or class used for QoS matching.
parameterHelp.name: Free-form name, ID, or number appropriate for the module.
parameterHelp.default: Placeholder. This value is replaced by your input in the module.
